{{Quote|Dealing with a Caridan merchant is a lot like trading with a nomadic water-seller on Beheboth. You'll get what you pay for, but only just, and you'd better check your purchase for sand lice.|[[Myrette Davani]]|<ref name="Carida: Heavy Duty">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030904carida|text=Carida: Heavy Duty|int=Carida: Heavy Duty}}</ref>}}
The '''Caridan''' were a mammalian species of [[Carida/Legends|Carida]]. Average Caridans stood over two meters tall and possessed dense musculatures due to Carida's high gravity level. They spoke [[Caridan language|Caridan]] and most also spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. Caridan society was structured around a mercantile culture led by an active market and Caridan owned industrial firms. In fact, the Caridans were widely known for their appraisal and bartering skills. The Caridans cooperated with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and later the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in the operation of the [[Carida Academy/Legends|Academy of Carida]]. The Empire's [[Speciesism|xenophobia]] ignored the Caridans outside the Imperial compound, although tensions grew after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Many Caridans vanished from the galaxy with the [[destruction of Carida]] at the hand of [[Kyp Durron]]. The few Caridans that survived were off-world as traders, [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] operatives, or fringers. Notable Caridans included [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] [[Furgan]] of Carida.
